task_completion.description: Create a mask isolating the cranial bone.
task_completion.visual_evidence: InVesalius interface showing a mask (colored overlay on the 2D slices) that highlights the skull bone, typically using a bone threshold preset.

task_completion.description: Include the internal brain cavity in the mask while excluding external soft tissues.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The mask overlay on the 2D slices should appear completely filled inside the skull (covering the brain area), but the external soft tissues (skin/face) must remain unmasked.

task_completion.description: Generate the 3D surface of the solid cranium.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D viewer pane in InVesalius showing a 3D model of the skull that appears solid (the large opening at the base or any holes are filled, representing the total cranial volume).

task_completion.description: Export the 3D surface as an STL file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An 'Export 3D surface' dialog box open in InVesalius with the format set to STL (and ideally binary if the option is visible).

task_completion.description: Save the STL file to the exact specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager, terminal window, or the save dialog confirming the file is named 'solid_cranium.stl' and is located in the '/home/ga/Documents/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the InVesalius 3 GUI to process the DICOM and generate the model.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show active interaction with the InVesalius 3 interface (e.g., clicking threshold tools, morphological operations, or surface creation buttons).

integrity.description: The solid model was genuinely created from the loaded DICOM data.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be a visible progression from the initial loaded scan to the masked bone, the filled cavity, and finally the 3D surface, rather than just importing a pre-made 3D model.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the software to create a fake output file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal should not show commands like 'touch /home/ga/Documents/solid_cranium.stl' or copying an unrelated file to mock the final output.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The correct output path is exactly '/home/ga/Documents/solid_cranium.stl'. The DICOM series used is 'ct_cranium/0051'. A correct solid cranial model must visually include the bone and the internal brain space (filled), but strictly exclude the external facial/skin soft tissues (which would appear as a face/head outline if incorrectly included).
